$comment: CANONICAL SOURCE OF TRUTH for every three.ws MCP directory listing. Regenerate with build-mcp-listing-source.mjs — do NOT hand-edit this JSON; edit the OVERLAY in that script. Factual fields (name/title/description/version/connect/tags-from-manifest) are read live from server*.json so listings can never drift from the manifests.

fieldConsumers.name: Official registry name; Glama/PulseMCP match this to the registry entry.
fieldConsumers.title: Display name on Smithery, mcp.so, LobeHub cards.
fieldConsumers.tagline: One-line summary for Smithery/mcp.so/LobeHub card subtitle.
fieldConsumers.description: Long description body on every directory.
fieldConsumers.category: Smithery + LobeHub category; mcp.so tag group.
fieldConsumers.connect: Install/connect snippet — remote URL for Smithery URL-publish; `npx` for stdio config JSON.
fieldConsumers.examplePrompts: LobeHub + mcp.so "example usage"; Smithery README examples.

servers.name: io.github.nirholas/threews-3d-studio-free
servers.title: three.ws 3D Studio (free)
servers.description: Free text/image → 3D. Five tools, no auth, no payment: forge_free (text → textured GLB), text_to_avatar (text/image → avatar), mesh_forge (art-directed mesh), rig_mesh (auto-rig a GLB for animation), forge_avatar (one-call text → rigged avatar). Results render in an interactive viewer.
servers.tagline: Free text/image → 3D. Five tools, no auth, no payment — forge meshes and rigged avatars on the house.
examplePrompts: Free: forge a 3D model of a mushroom house from text.
Free: make a rigged 3D avatar of an astronaut in one step.
Free: auto-rig this GLB for animation.
